title: Elbaf’s Warriors Turn Dark
description: On pages 13–14, this part follows Unknown person #13, Usopp, King Harald, Jarul, Unknown person #14, Giants as Elbaf’s warriors, Jarul, and the younger giants clash over the future of the island while dark forces twist pride and tradition into violence.
scene: Elbaf’s warriors, Jarul, and the younger giants clash over the future of the island while dark forces twist pride and tradition into violence.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We couldn't speak freely in the Holy Land.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Now that we're both in this country, I really want to talk to him...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That day, the massacre at the Castle took place...

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: HARald the benevolent, lost his life...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Let us out! Who closed the gate?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The forbidden Devil Fruit passed down within ElBAF!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But just how did this man come to be so highly regarded by his people?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you asked anyone what the scariest thing on the sea is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: The answer would always be the same...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That was how things were in that era...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In that age, even ships from the land of the giants...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: ...evoked immense fear due to Elbaf's reputation!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's a beautiful princess in your castle! Let's have a peek!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Bara Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha Ha!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Harald began his reign at 45 years of age - just 15 in human terms. Despite this, King Harald...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Go on! Tell me who your God is?!

summary: This part advances the The Giants Turn on Their Legend through 37 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
